69d31a538c78661dd8b37baf.reference_summary: A joke about the daily cyclical lifestyle of someone who likes coffee and alcohol. The sequence runs through waking up and caffeinating, trying to recover by hydrating, getting drunk again that night, and then repeating the whole routine the next day.
69d31a538c78661dd8b37baf.tone_notes: Keep it dry, matter-of-fact, and a little self-aware. The joke works best when the routine is described like a perfectly normal life cycle instead of something dramatic.

69d1a3fb8c78661dd8f611bb.reference_summary: A riff on the popular 'Everyone watches women's sports' apparel line, especially familiar to NWSL and WNBA fans. This version turns the original pro-women's-sports message into a sharper rebuttal by saying it doesn't care about men's sports at all.

68a60ecc6fbe8b1fbcc405cc.reference_summary: A 90s Honda Del Sol reference with a play on 'soul searching' turned into 'Del Sol searchin''.

etsy_2755902323.reference_summary: Part of the 'Coloradans Against' joke format: a snarky rebuttal to the Colorado stereotype that everyone is into triathlons.

etsy_2613432644.reference_summary: Part of the 'Coloradans Against' series: a sarcastic anti-Colorado joke aimed at the local obsession with climbing fourteeners, mountains over 14,000 feet.

etsy_2649334247.reference_summary: Part of the 'Coloradans Against' series: a sarcastic anti-Colorado joke pushing back on the state's craft beer stereotype.
target_audience: people tired of craft beer culture

etsy_2598077934.reference_summary: Part of the 'Coloradans Against' series: a sarcastic anti-Colorado joke aimed at the stereotype that everyone in the state is obsessed with hiking.

5d89cc2120517626420dd572.reference_summary: A Point Break reference to Bells Beach, Australia, the climactic surf destination in the movie where the criminal-surfer storyline comes to a head.
5d89cc2120517626420dd572.tone_notes: Confident and specific. Let the reference do the work and avoid turning it into a plot summary.

5d8e158b1361511e3eb8ed92.reference_summary: An Airplane! reference that riffs on 'Jesus is my copilot' shirts by swapping in Roger, the movie's copilot character played by Kareem Abdul-Jabbar.

5d89cc1fb4b8c76e3917085f.reference_summary: A reference to DEVO's song 'Whip It' designed to resemble a no parking sign, turning the lyric into a visual street-sign joke.

5d89cc02162c5f464e737139.reference_summary: A mashup of two sayings: 'Beer before liquor, never sicker' and 'Red sky at night, sailor's delight,' turned into a drinking joke about sailors.
target_audience: people who like old sayings twisted into jokes
5d89cc02162c5f464e737139.tone_notes: Wry and casually cutting. Sound amused, not cute.

5d89cbe26bbdbb2e6a46975e.reference_summary: A Breaking Bad parody that mimics the show's periodic-table title styling and turns it into a fart joke.

5d89cc1ff937647d81ffbd7b.reference_summary: A pun on the phrase 'Support the arts' that turns it into an appeal to support Art Garfunkel specifically.

5d89cbe0b4b8c76e3916d6e4.reference_summary: A toilet-humor twist on the phrase 'I've got the body that rocks the party,' changing it to 'rocks the potty.'
target_audience: people who like dumb phrase twists

5d89cc21162c5f464e739141.reference_summary: A joke on the goodbye phrase 'smell you later,' with an added asparagus joke because asparagus famously makes your pee smell; the phrase also shows up in Fresh Prince of Bel-Air pop culture.

5d89cc11b4b8c76e39170242.reference_summary: A cycling joke about how long rides and bike seats can cause numbness in the perineum.

5d89cbe26bbdbb2e6a469828.reference_summary: A reference to the 2008 USA men's 4x100 medley relay team, listing the first names of the four swimmers including Michael Phelps.

5d89cc2e20517626420dd8b0.reference_summary: A Ghostbusters reference to the line 'There is no Dana, only Zuul' after Dana Barrett is possessed by Zuul.

5d89cbe020517626420dadda.reference_summary: A snarky reversal of 'guns don't kill people' slogans, taking the phrase literally and pointing to the chain of human decisions behind how a violent person gets a gun.

5d89cc12f937647d81ffb601.reference_summary: A deep-cut reference to The Electric Horseman, joking that the rescued show horse Rising Star would never have survived after being released into the wild.

5d89cc1fb4b8c76e39170868.reference_summary: A Total Recall reference built around the repeated line 'Get your ass to Mars,' with the movie's Mars-setting and alien-atmosphere climax in mind.

5d89cbe2162c5f464e735a5f.reference_summary: A skiing-culture joke built on the idea that skiing in jeans is something only a clueless non-skier would do, so recommending it becomes an insult disguised as advice.
target_audience: people who like insider sports jokes
5d89cbe2162c5f464e735a5f.tone_notes: Play it like a knowingly bad recommendation. The humor works because real skiers recognize immediately that this is uncool.

5d89cbdef937647d81ff9696.reference_summary: A mashup of the familiar Windows 'Ctrl + Alt + Delete' keyboard shortcut and the alt-country music genre, swapping 'Delete' for 'Country' to make a music-nerd tech pun.
5d89cbdef937647d81ff9696.tone_notes: Keep it dry and a little nerdy. The joke lands best when treated like a neat crossover rather than overexplained.

5d89cc026bbdbb2e6a46ab51.reference_summary: A wordplay joke on the bragging format 'I put the X in Y,' using the silent B in 'subtle' to boast about being subtle even though the letter itself is already hidden.
target_audience: people who like dry, nerdy humor
5d89cc026bbdbb2e6a46ab51.tone_notes: Matter-of-fact and lightly smug. The joke works because the brag contradicts itself in a very understated way.

5d89cc03b4b8c76e3916f406.reference_summary: A gross-out Christmas parody of the 'Dirty Sanchez' slang term, turning it into 'Dirty Santa-Chez' and giving Santa a brown mustache for the visual punch line.
5d89cc03b4b8c76e3916f406.tone_notes: Lean into the shamelessly juvenile shock of it. Keep the copy playful and irreverent rather than clinical.

5d89cc30f937647d81ffc562.reference_summary: A bathroom-humor gambling joke that mimics a casino craps table, but swaps in text and design cues suggesting someone has literally just crapped themselves.
target_audience: people who like visual pun shirts
5d89cc30f937647d81ffc562.tone_notes: Treat it like dumb, committed visual wordplay. The humor comes from recognizing the craps-table format and realizing how far the joke takes it.

5d89cc3020517626420ddafa.reference_summary: A movie reference to The Color of Money, where the main character Vince wears this shirt prominently while hustling and playing pool throughout the film.
target_audience: Paul Newman and Tom Cruise movie fans
5d89cc3020517626420ddafa.tone_notes: Confident and specific, like a nod between people who know the film. Let the reference carry the appeal without overselling it.

5d89cc2013615160bf6ed3ae.reference_summary: A parody of the NBA logo, using the same red-and-blue sports branding style but replacing the basketball player silhouette with someone on a bike holding a rake, as if it stood for the fictional National Bike Jousting Association.
target_audience: people who like absurd fake sports jokes
5d89cc2013615160bf6ed3ae.tone_notes: Play the fake-league premise straight. The joke lands because it looks official while describing a completely ridiculous sport.

5d89cbe020517626420dade5.reference_summary: A movie reference to Romancing the Stone, where El Tenedor del Diablo is a landmark on the treasure map that helps lead to the hidden jewel.
5d89cbe020517626420dade5.tone_notes: Treat it like a knowing wink for people who remember the map and the treasure hunt. Specificity helps more than hype here.

5d89cc14f937647d81ffb6f2.reference_summary: A joke on the phrase 'See you later, alligator' that swaps in 'crocodile' instead, leaning on how people constantly confuse alligators and crocodiles; adding a sheep as a third unrelated animal pushes the absurdity even further.
target_audience: people who like deliberately wrong jokes
5d89cc14f937647d81ffb6f2.tone_notes: Play up the committed wrongness of it. The joke is that the phrase is broken on purpose and then made even weirder.

5d89cc1f1cbf3a25d594f159.reference_summary: A bar-ordering joke that uses the phrase 'Make mine a...' but replaces a normal cocktail order with a Molotov cocktail, treating an incendiary weapon like a drink request.
target_audience: people who like absurd violent wordplay
5d89cc1f1cbf3a25d594f159.tone_notes: Deliver it like a casual drink order. The humor comes from how calmly it treats something nobody would ever actually order at a bar.

5d89cc13b4b8c76e3917033d.reference_summary: A deadpan archery joke that claims only 50% of archery is aiming, even though aiming is obviously most of the whole activity, leaving the reader to wonder what absurdly large missing half remains.
target_audience: people who like deliberately bad logic jokes
5d89cc13b4b8c76e3917033d.tone_notes: Say it with total confidence. The joke works because the statement feels weirdly precise while making no real sense.

5d89cc1213615160bf6ec860.reference_summary: A gross wordplay twist on 'Caution: Wet Paint' that swaps in 'taint,' using the rhyme to turn a normal warning sign into a joke about a wet crotch.
target_audience: people who like crude sign-parody jokes
5d89cc1213615160bf6ec860.tone_notes: Keep it punchy and juvenile. The joke works because it mimics a familiar warning exactly long enough to spring the vulgar swap.

5d89cc026bbdbb2e6a46abac.reference_summary: A reference to Fletch, where the main character abuses someone else's country club account and orders, 'I'll have a Bloody Mary, a steak sandwich, and a steak sandwich.'
5d89cc026bbdbb2e6a46abac.tone_notes: Treat it like a specific wink to people who know the scene. The fun is in the oddly exact order and the character's shamelessness.

5d89cc021cbf3a25d594dacc.reference_summary: A Monopoly-inspired joke on 'Community Chest,' turning the game term into a boob joke by placing chest icons over the breasts as if her chest is being shared with the whole community.
5d89cc021cbf3a25d594dacc.tone_notes: Keep it playful and winkingly immature. The joke works because it starts from a familiar board-game phrase and immediately turns literal.

5d89cbe01cbf3a25d594c3bf.reference_summary: A joke that combines the map phrase 'You are here' with the phrase 'shit list,' so the reader's marked location is that they are currently on the wearer's shit list.
target_audience: people who like hostile sign jokes
5d89cbe01cbf3a25d594c3bf.tone_notes: Keep it blunt and dry. The humor comes from turning a neutral wayfinding graphic into a petty personal insult.

5d89cc1313615160bf6ec92e.reference_summary: A fart joke built on the slang term 'cropdusting,' where someone walks through an area farting like a cropduster plane spraying a field; saying it out loud makes the usually sneaky act shamelessly explicit.
target_audience: people who like crude slang jokes
5d89cc1313615160bf6ec92e.tone_notes: Lean into the brazen admission. The joke works because it's normally something done secretly, but the shirt announces it proudly.

5d89cc3020517626420ddb2e.reference_summary: A political-insult joke aimed at demands to teach the Bible in science class, arguing by analogy that Aesop's Fables would make just as much sense there since both are non-scientific stories with moral lessons and fantastical elements.
target_audience: people who like argumentative slogan shirts
5d89cc3020517626420ddb2e.tone_notes: Keep it sharp and skeptical rather than preachy. The joke works by exposing the inconsistency through an obviously absurd comparison.

5d89cbf213615160bf6eb374.reference_summary: A literary twist on the Glengarry Glen Ross line 'Coffee is for closers,' swapping coffee for Franz Kafka to make a dry joke about high-pressure sales swagger colliding with serious 20th-century literature.
target_audience: people who like smart pop-culture mashups
5d89cbf213615160bf6eb374.tone_notes: Play it as a smart, understated mashup. The joke works best when it sounds like a line that should almost make sense.

5d8e1581b4b8c76220050334.reference_summary: A double-entendre joke on 'knowing someone in the biblical sense,' which can mean having sex with them; applying that phrase to Jesus sounds innocent at first because he appears throughout the Bible, but the alternate meaning makes it absurdly inappropriate.
target_audience: people who like irreverent religious humor
5d8e1581b4b8c76220050334.tone_notes: Keep it dry and let the layered meaning do the work. The joke lands because the phrase sounds harmless until the second meaning clicks.

5d89cc13162c5f464e738725.reference_summary: A Spanglish twist on the phrase 'There's no I in team,' swapping in the Spanish word 'yo' to make a bilingual version of the same joke.
5d89cc13162c5f464e738725.tone_notes: Keep it breezy and straightforward. The joke is simple and lands from the quick Spanglish substitution.

5d89cc041cbf3a25d594dcaf.reference_summary: A parody of The North Face logo that swaps 'North' for 'Gas,' combining the outdoor-brand look with the slang term 'the gas face,' a disgusted or disrespectful expression showing strong disapproval.
5d89cc041cbf3a25d594dcaf.tone_notes: Keep it confident and a little dismissive. The joke works because the familiar brand format makes the slang hit harder.

5d89cc126bbdbb2e6a46b6a5.reference_summary: A dark joke built around the clinical term 'priapism,' with the tagline 'What's all the excitement about?' using 'excitement' as a euphemism for sexual arousal while pretending the situation is much more casual than it obviously is.
target_audience: people who like clinical-word crude jokes
5d89cc126bbdbb2e6a46b6a5.tone_notes: Keep it dry and understated. The joke works because the clinical term and euphemistic tagline both downplay something that is obviously sexual, awkward, and urgent.

5d89cc20162c5f464e739133.reference_summary: A movie reference to Any Which Way but Loose, where Clint Eastwood's pet orangutan Clyde sticks his arm out the window to signal a right turn, turning a normal driving gesture into a weirdly memorable visual gag.
target_audience: Any Which Way but Loose fans
5d89cc20162c5f464e739133.tone_notes: Treat it like a fond, specific callback. The humor comes from how oddly memorable and ridiculous the Clyde turn-signal bit is.

5d89cc02162c5f464e7370e3.reference_summary: A bathroom-joke twist on the phrase 'do me a solid,' turning 'solid' into a literal poop reference instead of a favor.

5d89cc1420517626420dceae.reference_summary: A joke aimed at the misattributed 'Beer is proof that God loves us' quote often pinned on Benjamin Franklin, reframing beer as a product of science rather than divinity.
target_audience: people who like debunking famous quotes
5d89cc1420517626420dceae.tone_notes: Dry, sharp, irreverent, and a little smug. Lean into skeptical barstool-smart snark without sounding angry or juvenile.

5d8e15701cbf3a56cc25fe29.reference_summary: A dark environmental joke that contrasts a cheerful 'Happy Earth Day' slogan with the obviously harmful act of burning tires.

5d89cc12162c5f464e73869c.reference_summary: A joke at the expense of lacrosse players, implying they would rather play baseball but switched because they cannot hit a curveball.
